title: GoPro Deals So Good You'll Jump off a Cliff to Get Them
gist: WIRED Prime Day action camera and 360 camera deals guide covering GoPro, Insta360, DJI, and Olympus. The GoPro Max 2 at $299 (40% off) is the top pick. DJI Osmo 360 is highlighted for its tight integration with the DJI ecosystem including direct pairing with the DJI Mic 3 wireless microphone system.

themes.theme: Action Camera Market: GoPro vs. DJI vs. Insta360 Competition

insights.claim: GoPro Max 2 at $299 (40% off from $500) is WIRED's top 360 camera pick, offering True 8K 10-bit video from dual 14mm lens sensors with 10-bit log color capability—a feature the author notes 'you'd be hard-pressed to find in other 360 cameras.'
insights.speaker: Scott Gilbertson
insights.so_what: GoPro's 40% Prime Day markdown on its flagship 360 camera—matching the Insta360 X5 at 21% off—suggests GoPro is using aggressive pricing to defend its 360 camera market position against Insta360's competitive offerings.
insights.quote: By far the best camera deal this Prime Day is GoPro's Max 2 360 camera, which is currently 40 percent off.

insights.claim: DJI Osmo 360 camera integrates directly with the DJI Mic 3 wireless microphone system, supports frame rates and codecs compatible with DJI drone footage, and uses DJI's magnetic quick-release accessory system—positioning it as the 360 camera for existing DJI ecosystem users.
insights.speaker: Scott Gilbertson
insights.so_what: DJI's strategy of deep ecosystem integration across cameras, mics, and drones creates switching costs for professional creators and prosumers—a lock-in dynamic relevant to DJI's platform value as it defends market share against GoPro and Insta360.
insights.quote: integrates tightly into the DJI ecosystem, pairing directly with the DJI Mic 3, and offering frame rates and codecs that will mix well with drone footage

insights.claim: The Insta360 Ace Pro 2, WIRED's top vlogging action camera, lacks 10-bit color support—described by the editor as 'the big downside'—while the GoPro Max 2 supports 10-bit log color, giving GoPro a post-production flexibility advantage over Insta360 in the prosumer market.
insights.speaker: Scott Gilbertson
insights.so_what: 10-bit color as a differentiator between GoPro and Insta360 is a prosumer-tier capability gap; as more creators demand color-grading flexibility, this technical advantage could influence market share at the high end.
insights.quote: The big downside to the Ace Pro 2 is that it doesn't support 10-bit color, which means you have fewer options to colorize footage in post-production.
